description: Professional hospital UI redesign: medical teal color palette, redesigned sidebar with branding, enhanced dashboard cards, improved tables with zebra striping and hover effects, rounded pill status badges, updated login page, and improved empty states. All changes verified visually via Playwright screenshots.

asi.hypothesis: Changing the UI to use a medical teal/cyan color palette and improving visual hierarchy would make the app look significantly more professional and hospital-like
visual_changes: Global theme: teal primary color, medical palette, improved dark mode
Sidebar: HeartPulse icon, Sistema Hospitalario subtitle, active indicators, better grouping
Dashboard: colored stat cards with left border accents, themed list icons, hover shadows
Tables: zebra striping, rounded borders, better headers, improved hover
Status badges: rounded pills with dot indicators
Login: teal background panel matching medical theme
Topbar: glassmorphism backdrop blur, primary colored mobile logo
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 2: Improved table action buttons with colored hover states (teal/amber/red), enhanced CTA buttons with shadows, redesigned search/filter controls in card containers, segmented-control style filter pills on encounters page, and colored compliance summary cards with icon containers.

asi.hypothesis: Adding colored hover states to action buttons, improving filter controls with card containers and segmented control styling, and enhancing compliance cards with colored backgrounds will make the UI feel more responsive and professional
visual_changes: Table action buttons: teal hover for view, amber for edit, red for delete
CTA buttons: shadow-sm and improved gap for better presence
Search bars: unified card container with borderless input
Filter pills: segmented control style with active/inactive states
Compliance cards: colored backgrounds with icon containers
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 3: Added card shadows and rounded corners to global Card component, improved dashboard list items with larger rounded icon containers, enhanced empty state with dashed border and card background, improved DataTable pagination with card styling and hover states on page buttons.

asi.hypothesis: Adding subtle shadows and rounded corners to cards, improving icon containers in list items, and polishing empty states/pagination will give the UI more depth and a more finished professional feel
visual_changes: Card component: rounded-md, shadow-sm, border-border/60 instead of ring-1
Dashboard list items: larger size-10 icon containers with rounded-md and shadow-sm
Empty state: dashed border, card background, larger icon container
DataTable pagination: card container with shadow, hover states on buttons
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 4: Added subtle rounded corners to all global interactive elements — Button (rounded-sm base, rounded-md icon buttons), Input (rounded-sm), and Select trigger/popup (rounded-sm/rounded-md). Removed harsh angular edges across the entire application while maintaining a professional medical aesthetic.

asi.hypothesis: Replacing angular rounded-none with subtle rounded-sm on buttons, inputs, and selects will make the entire UI feel more modern, approachable, and professionally polished without losing the medical aesthetic
visual_changes: Button base: rounded-none → rounded-sm (subtle 2px rounding on all buttons)
Button xs/sm sizes: rounded-none → rounded-sm
Icon buttons (icon-xs/icon-sm/icon-lg): rounded-none → rounded-md (more rounding for square buttons)
Input: rounded-none → rounded-sm
Select trigger: rounded-none → rounded-sm
Select popup: rounded-none → rounded-md + shadow-lg (stronger shadow for dropdowns)
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 5: Refined sidebar navigation with left accent bar + rounded dot indicator, elevated topbar with shadow-sm and backdrop-blur-md, improved page header with stronger shadow and backdrop blur, and softened skeleton loading states with rounded-sm and muted/70.

asi.hypothesis: Adding a left accent bar to sidebar active items, refining the active indicator dot, adding subtle shadows to topbar and page headers, and softening skeleton states will improve visual hierarchy and make the UI feel more polished and responsive
visual_changes: Sidebar active item: left accent bar (w-0.5 h-5) + rounded-full dot indicator
Topbar: shadow-sm + backdrop-blur-md + bg-card/80 for better elevation
Page header: shadow-sm + backdrop-blur-md + border-border/60
Skeleton: rounded-sm + bg-muted/70 for softer loading states
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 6: Unified toolbar cards for patients and encounters pages (grouping search, filters, sort into single card containers), and added hover lift effects (-translate-y-px + shadow-md) to dashboard stat cards and quick access items.

asi.hypothesis: Grouping all table controls into unified toolbar cards and adding subtle hover lift effects to dashboard cards will make data-heavy pages feel more organized and add tactile polish to the interface
visual_changes: Patients toolbar: unified card container with all controls inside
Encounters toolbar: unified card container with status filters, site select, and search
Dashboard stat cards: hover:-translate-y-px hover:shadow-md transition
Dashboard quick access items: hover lift effect with shadow-md
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 7: Semantic colored left borders on dashboard stat cards (teal/sky/amber/slate matching card meaning), subtle gradient background on DataTable header, and pill backgrounds on sidebar navigation group labels.

asi.hypothesis: Adding semantic colored borders to stat cards will make the dashboard more visually scannable, a gradient table header adds subtle depth, and pill backgrounds on sidebar group labels improve navigation organization
visual_changes: Dashboard stat cards: distinct colored left borders matching semantic meaning (teal, sky, amber, slate)
DataTable header: gradient from-muted/90 to-muted/70 replacing flat bg-muted/80
Sidebar group labels: pill background with sidebar-accent/40 instead of plain text
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 8: Added left-border hover accent to DataTable rows, enhanced status badges with colored borders and shadow-sm in encounters, and standardized clinical document badges as rounded-full pills with shadow-sm for consistency.

asi.hypothesis: Adding a left-border hover accent to table rows will make data tables feel more interactive, and standardizing badges with colored borders and shadow-sm will improve visual consistency across the app
visual_changes: DataTable rows: border-l-2 border-transparent with hover:border-l-primary/40 (or muted)
Encounters status badges: colored semantic borders + shadow-sm
Clinical documents badges: rounded-full + shadow-sm + px-2 padding for pill shape
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 9: Unified toolbar card for clinical documents filters (status + type selects), and added bg-card background to DataTable container for subtle elevation across all tables.

asi.hypothesis: Applying the unified toolbar card pattern to clinical documents and adding bg-card to table containers will improve visual consistency and depth across all data-heavy pages
visual_changes: Clinical documents: unified toolbar card with rounded-lg border bg-card shadow-sm
Clinical documents: select triggers use bg-background for hierarchy
DataTable container: bg-card background for subtle elevation
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 10: Added icon prop to PageHeader component with colored background circle, and applied it to Patients (Users icon), Encounters (ClipboardList icon), and Clinical Documents (FileText icon) pages for visual identity.

asi.hypothesis: Adding distinctive icons with colored backgrounds to page headers will give each page a stronger visual identity and make the interface feel more branded and polished
visual_changes: PageHeader: new icon + iconBgClass props rendering a colored icon circle
Patients page: Users icon in teal circle
Encounters page: ClipboardList icon in teal circle
Clinical Documents page: FileText icon in teal circle
asi.lint_status: Modified files pass ultracite check after auto-fix
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 11: Applied PageHeader icon pattern to all major list pages: Consents (ShieldCheck), Medication Orders (Pill), Service Requests (FlaskConical), Interconsultations (MessageSquare), Regulatory Tasks (Gavel in amber), and Patient Requests (Copy in sky).

asi.hypothesis: Applying the page header icon pattern consistently across all major list pages will create a unified visual identity system where every page has distinctive but consistent branding
visual_changes: Consents: ShieldCheck icon in teal circle
Medication orders: Pill icon in teal circle
Service requests: FlaskConical icon in teal circle
Interconsultations: MessageSquare icon in teal circle
Regulatory tasks: Gavel icon in amber circle
Patient requests: Copy icon in sky circle
asi.lint_status: All files pass ultracite check after auto-fix
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 12: Added distinctive icons to dashboard system status cards: Server (emerald) for API, Database (sky) for DB, Shield (amber) for Auth, FileOutput (violet) for RIPS. Each card now shows a colored icon container next to the label.

asi.hypothesis: Adding semantic colored icons to the system status cards will make the dashboard's system status section feel more polished and consistent with the icon-rich design system
visual_changes: API card: Server icon in emerald container
Database card: Database icon in sky container
Auth card: Shield icon in amber container
RIPS card: FileOutput icon in violet container
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 13: Added rounded-sm and shadow-sm to sidebar brand icon containers (both expanded and collapsed states) and to the sidebar collapse button for visual consistency with the app's rounded design language.

asi.hypothesis: Rounding the sidebar brand icon containers and collapse button will eliminate the last remaining harsh angular edges in the navigation, making the entire interface feel more cohesive
visual_changes: Sidebar brand icon (expanded): rounded-sm + shadow-sm
Sidebar brand icon (collapsed): rounded-sm + shadow-sm
Sidebar collapse button: rounded-sm
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 14: Removed TanStack Router Devtools badge and React Query Devtools button from __root.tsx to eliminate development-only UI elements that were visible on every page, improving production professionalism.

asi.hypothesis: Removing development-only devtools badges from the production UI will make the application look significantly more professional and hospital-ready
visual_changes: Removed TanStackRouterDevtools badge from bottom-left of all pages
Removed ReactQueryDevtools button from bottom-right of all pages
asi.tsc_status: No TypeScript errors in modified files

description: Iteration 15: Rounded the login page brand icon containers (rounded-sm + shadow-sm) and added shadow-sm to sign-in/sign-up submit buttons for subtle elevation, matching the polished design language of authenticated pages.

asi.hypothesis: Rounding the login page brand icons and adding shadow to auth buttons will make the login page feel as polished as the rest of the app, eliminating the last visual inconsistency between public and authenticated pages
visual_changes: Login brand icon (desktop): rounded-sm + shadow-sm
Login brand icon (mobile): rounded-sm + shadow-sm
SignInForm submit button: shadow-sm
SignUpForm submit button: shadow-sm

description: Iteration 16: Standardized PageHeader rounding by changing icon container, back button, and keyboard shortcut badge from rounded-md to rounded-sm for consistency with the app's rounded-sm design language.

asi.hypothesis: Aligning all PageHeader interactive elements with rounded-sm instead of rounded-md will eliminate a subtle inconsistency in the rounding scale across the interface
visual_changes: PageHeader icon container: rounded-md → rounded-sm
PageHeader back button: rounded-md → rounded-sm
PageHeader keyboard shortcut badge: rounded-md → rounded-sm

description: Iteration 17: Standardized sidebar rounding by changing nav items and group label pills from rounded-md to rounded-sm, and added rounded-full to the collapsed sidebar footer indicator dot.

asi.hypothesis: Applying rounded-sm consistently throughout the sidebar will make the navigation feel more cohesive with the rest of the interface
visual_changes: Sidebar nav items: rounded-md → rounded-sm
Sidebar group labels: rounded-md → rounded-sm
Collapsed sidebar footer dot: added rounded-full

description: Iteration 18: Enhanced sidebar active item visibility by increasing background opacity (10% → 15%), accent bar width (0.5 → 1) with rounded-full, and indicator dot size (1.5 → 2).

asi.hypothesis: Making the sidebar active state more visually prominent will improve navigation usability and make the current location more immediately obvious to users
visual_changes: Active background: bg-sidebar-primary/10 → bg-sidebar-primary/15
Accent bar: w-0.5 → w-1 with rounded-full
Indicator dot: size-1.5 → size-2
